summary: Iteration 10 (pure refactor validation): Ran full existing backend test suite (33 tests) after E1 extracted shared helper functions in server.py. All 33/33 tests PASSED in 6.73s — no behavioral regression from the refactor. Byte-identical outputs preserved for baseline schedule (req=40/ass=38/short=2), auto-plan (req=66/ass=65/short=1 across 9 lines), suggest-lines (free_pool=38, top-3 at 100% coverage), and fill-shortages (unfillable short=2 leaves state unchanged).
critical_code_review_comments: Refactor is behavior-preserving — 33/33 existing tests pass with no changes needed to test suite.

The `is None` / `is not None` usages in server.py (lines 108, 125) and backend_test.py (lines 121, 347, 501) are idiomatic Python and correctly match the reviewer's rule 'Keep is only for None checks' — no action needed.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seeded state (persons=76, details=72, lines=15).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 10 was a pure refactor by E1 to reduce cyclomatic complexity via extracted helpers (_person_full_name, _names_from_ids, _apply_override_picks, _select_eligible, _build_work_items, _skill_totals, _group_details_by_line, _pick_specialists, _simulate_line_fill, _collect_used_person_ids, _build_suggestion). All 33 tests still pass byte-identically to iteration 9. The regression suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py comprehensively covers baseline schedule, auto-plan (with/without absentees), suggest-lines, fill-shortages, adjust set/clear, export, analytics, stats, and shift isolation. Safe to continue further refactors as long as this suite keeps passing.
